title: sqlite3_profile gives milliseconds (was nanoseconds in earlier releases)
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, value FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('Test2', NULL);
SELECT id, name, value, created_at FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
UPDATE test_table SET value = CAST(value AS SIGNED) WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: sqlite3_profile returns milliseconds instead of nanoseconds
